root_causes.cause: R2 Serial0/1/0 is configured shutdown (administratively down), causing its oper_state to be down.
evidence: R2 running-config: Serial0/1/0 contains "shutdown" (get_running_config for R2).
R2 interface-status: admin_state="down", oper_state="down", last_change="2026-05-26T09:26:53.000925+00:00" (get_interface_status for R2).
R2 OSPF: Serial0/1/0 interface_state = Down (get_ospf for R2).

root_causes.cause: R1 Serial0/1/0 is administratively up but reports oper_state=lower-layer-down due to loss of link/carrier from the remote end — consistent with R2 being administratively down.
evidence: R1 interface-status: admin_state="up", oper_state="lower-layer-down", last_change="2026-05-26T09:26:56.000331+00:00" (get_interface_status for R1).
R1 running-config: Serial0/1/0 configured with ip 10.0.0.1/30 (peer IP on R2 is 10.0.0.2/30) — IPs match expected peer but link shows no carrier (get_running_config for R1).
R1 OSPF: Serial0/1/0 interface_state = Down (get_ospf for R1).
Event timing: R2 last_change 09:26:53, R1 last_change 09:26:56 (timestamps indicate R2 state change preceded R1 loss of carrier).

root_causes.cause: R2 Serial0/1/0 is configured shutdown (administratively down), causing its oper_state to be down.
evidence: R2 running-config: Serial0/1/0 contains "shutdown" (get_running_config for R2).
R2 interface-status: admin_state="down", oper_state="down", last_change="2026-05-26T09:26:53.000925+00:00" (get_interface_status for R2).
R2 OSPF: Serial0/1/0 interface_state = Down (get_ospf for R2).

root_causes.cause: R1 Serial0/1/0 is administratively up but reports oper_state=lower-layer-down due to loss of link/carrier from the remote end — consistent with R2 being administratively down.

root_causes.cause: R1 Serial0/1/0 is administratively up but reports oper_state=lower-layer-down due to loss of link/carrier from the remote end — consistent with R2 being administratively down.
evidence: R1 interface-status: admin_state="up", oper_state="lower-layer-down", last_change="2026-05-26T09:26:56.000331+00:00" (get_interface_status for R1).
R1 running-config: Serial0/1/0 configured with ip 10.0.0.1/30 (peer IP on R2 is 10.0.0.2/30) — IPs match expected peer but link shows no carrier (get_running_config for R1).
R1 OSPF: Serial0/1/0 interface_state = Down (get_ospf for R1).
Event timing: R2 last_change 09:26:53, R1 last_change 09:26:56 (timestamps indicate R2 state change preceded R1 loss of carrier).

data.intent_description: R2 Serial0/1/0 is administratively down causing its oper_state=down and R1 Serial0/1/0 to report lower-layer-down; goal is to restore the link so both interfaces return to expected up/up and OSPF adjacency resumes.

plan.problem: R2 Serial0/1/0 is administratively shutdown, causing loss of carrier on the R1–R2 serial link and OSPF on that link to be Down.
plan.fix_summary: Re-enable interface Serial0/1/0 on router2 (remove administrative shutdown) so the link regains carrier and R1's oper_state returns to up.

parsed.intent_description: R2 Serial0/1/0 is administratively down causing its oper_state=down and R1 Serial0/1/0 to report lower-layer-down; goal is to restore the link so both interfaces return to expected up/up and OSPF adjacency resumes.

messages.content:
What was observed
- R2 Serial0/1/0: administratively down (admin=down, oper=down).  
- R1 Serial0/1/0: administratively up but oper=lower-layer-down.  
- OSPF on the R1–R2 serial link was Down on both routers.

Diagnosis (main cause)
- R2 Serial0/1/0 was configured with "shutdown" (administrative shutdown), causing loss of carrier and R1 reporting lower-layer-down. Root cause: administrative shutdown on R2.

Changes made
- Re-enabled interface Serial0/1/0 on R2 (removed/cleared shutdown — set admin_state=up).

Verification
- Passed. Post-change checks show:
  - R2 Serial0/1/0 admin=up, oper=up.
  - R1 Serial0/1/0 admin=up, oper=up.
  - OSPF adjacency on the link is full on both sides (neighbor_state=ospf-nbr-full; R2 sees neighbor_id 1.1.1.1, R1 sees neighbor_id 2.2.2.2).
  - R2 running-config no longer contains "shutdown" on Serial0/1/0.

Remaining issues
- None. The link and OSPF adjacency have been restored.

data.last_message:
What was observed
- R2 Serial0/1/0: administratively down (admin=down, oper=down).  
- R1 Serial0/1/0: administratively up but oper=lower-layer-down.  
- OSPF on the R1–R2 serial link was Down on both routers.

Diagnosis (main cause)
- R2 Serial0/1/0 was configured with "shutdown" (administrative shutdown), causing loss of carrier and R1 reporting lower-layer-down. Root cause: administrative shutdown on R2.

Changes made
- Re-enabled interface Serial0/1/0 on R2 (removed/cleared shutdown — set admin_state=up).

Verification
- Passed. Post-change checks show:
  - R2 Serial0/1/0 admin=up, oper=up.
  - R1 Serial0/1/0 admin=up, oper=up.
  - OSPF adjacency on the link is full on both sides (neighbor_state=ospf-nbr-full; R2 sees neighbor_id 1.1.1.1, R1 sees neighbor_id 2.2.2.2).
  - R2 running-config no longer contains "shutdown" on Serial0/1/0.

Remaining issues
- None. The link and OSPF adjacency have been restored.
